metadata.title: Guidance for Industry: Considerations for Developmental Toxicity Studies for Preventive and Therapeutic Vaccines for Infectious Disease Indications

rules.rule_text: DART studies are required for preventive and therapeutic vaccines intended for use in females of childbearing potential or pregnant individuals; childhood vaccines not typically subject to this guidance.
rules.verbatim_quote: This guidance addresses developmental toxicity studies for preventive and therapeutic vaccines for infectious disease indications. It does not address vaccines intended for use exclusively in children.
rules.exceptions: Vaccines exclusively intended for use in children are outside scope of this guidance.

rules.rule_text: For vaccines intended for maternal immunization (i.e., vaccination during pregnancy to protect the pregnant woman and/or her infant), DART data must be submitted and reviewed before enrolling pregnant women in clinical trials.
rules.source_section: IV. Timing of Developmental Toxicity Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: For vaccines intended for maternal immunization (e.g., vaccination during pregnancy to protect the pregnant woman and/or her infant), developmental toxicity data should be submitted to the IND and reviewed before pregnant women are enrolled in clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: For vaccines not intended for maternal immunization but targeting females of childbearing potential, clinical trials may proceed without prior DART data if adequate pregnancy precautions are implemented; however, DART studies must be completed and submitted with the BLA under 21 CFR 601.2.
rules.source_section: IV. Timing of Developmental Toxicity Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: For vaccines not intended for maternal immunization but intended for use in females of childbearing potential, clinical trials may proceed in the absence of developmental toxicity data if adequate precautions are taken to prevent pregnancy... developmental toxicity studies should be submitted with the Biologics License Application (BLA) (21 CFR 601.2).
rules.exceptions: Clinical trials may proceed without DART only when adequate pregnancy precautions (e.g., exclusion criteria, contraception requirements) are in place.

rules.rule_text: Male fertility studies are not required for vaccines in Phase I through Phase III clinical development.
rules.source_section: IV. Timing of Developmental Toxicity Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Male fertility studies are not needed to support Phases I through III clinical development of vaccines.
rules.exceptions: None noted; standard nonclinical reproductive toxicology for males is waived for vaccines.

rules.rule_text: The animal model selection for DART studies must be scientifically justified in the IND submission; the chosen species must be pharmacologically responsive to the vaccine antigen.
rules.verbatim_quote: The animal model used for developmental toxicity studies should be pharmacologically responsive to the vaccine antigen. Justification for the animal model selected should be provided in the IND.

rules.rule_text: Rat and rabbit are the standard species for DART studies; a minimum of 20 animals per group must be assigned to Caesarean section evaluation and a minimum of 20 animals per group to natural delivery, yielding at least 40 total animals per group.
rules.verbatim_quote: For rats and rabbits, at least 20 animals per group should be assigned to Caesarean section and at least 20 animals per group to natural delivery (i.e., at least 40 animals per group total).
rules.exceptions: Alternative species may be used with scientific justification if rats/rabbits are not pharmacologically responsive.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine dose administered to animals must equal one human dose regardless of body weight differences between the animal and human; allometric scaling is not applicable to vaccines.
rules.verbatim_quote: Because the immune response to a vaccine is not a simple dose-response relationship based on body weight, one human dose should equal one animal dose regardless of differences in body weight between humans and the test animal.

rules.rule_text: The dosing schedule in animals should mimic the clinical dosing schedule (episodic immunization); this is preferred over daily dosing to reflect the actual clinical exposure pattern.
rules.verbatim_quote: The dosing schedule in the animal should mimic, as closely as possible, the clinical dosing schedule (i.e., episodic immunization rather than daily dosing) to reflect the actual clinical exposure pattern.
rules.exceptions: Deviations from episodic dosing require scientific justification.

rules.rule_text: The route of administration in the animal DART study must match the intended clinical route of administration.
rules.verbatim_quote: The route of administration in the animal study should be the same as the intended clinical route of administration.

rules.rule_text: DART study dosing must expose animals from implantation through at least hard palate closure (organogenesis period) to assess teratogenic potential, and should continue through later gestational stages and the postnatal period to assess peri- and postnatal effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: The experimental procedure should cover the period from implantation through hard palate closure, and should continue through later stages of gestation and postnatal period to evaluate peri- and postnatal effects on the offspring.

rules.rule_text: Offspring must be followed through weaning to assess postnatal development and survival.
rules.verbatim_quote: Offspring should be followed through weaning to assess postnatal development and survival.

rules.rule_text: A placebo control group administered at the same schedule and frequency as the vaccine group must be included in the DART study design.
rules.verbatim_quote: A placebo control group, administered at the same schedule and frequency as the vaccine group, should be included in the study design.

rules.rule_text: When a novel adjuvant is included in the vaccine formulation, an adjuvant-only control group must be included in the DART study to characterize adjuvant-specific effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the vaccine contains a novel adjuvant, an adjuvant-only control group should be included to characterize adjuvant-specific developmental toxicity effects.
rules.exceptions: May not be required for well-characterized, previously studied adjuvants with established safety profiles.

rules.rule_text: If any non-antigen component of the vaccine (e.g., excipients, preservatives) causes adverse effects on development, additional control groups must be added to characterize the effect of that component.
rules.verbatim_quote: If adverse developmental effects are observed with non-antigen components of the vaccine (e.g., excipients, preservatives), additional control groups should be included to characterize the effects of these individual components.
rules.exceptions: Only triggered if adverse effects are observed with non-antigen components.

rules.rule_text: DART endpoints must include ICH S5A Stages C, D, and E: Stage C covers embryotoxicity/teratogenicity (organogenesis), Stage D covers perinatal and postnatal development, and Stage E covers fertility and early embryonic development if indicated.
rules.verbatim_quote: DART studies for vaccines should include endpoints described in ICH S5A Stages C, D, and E, as applicable. Stage C addresses embryo-fetal development (teratogenicity/organogenesis period); Stage D addresses peri- and postnatal development; Stage E addresses fertility and early embryonic development.
rules.exceptions: Stage E (fertility) is not required for males during Phase I-III.

rules.rule_text: Premating observations must be collected prior to vaccination to establish baseline health status of the dams.
rules.verbatim_quote: Premating observations should be collected to establish baseline health status of the animals prior to vaccination.

rules.rule_text: Gestational observations must include maternal body weight, food consumption, and clinical signs throughout gestation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Gestational observations should include maternal body weight, food consumption, and clinical signs throughout gestation.

rules.rule_text: Caesarean section evaluations must include maternal necropsy, fetal body weights, and external, visceral, and skeletal examinations of fetuses.
rules.verbatim_quote: Caesarean section evaluations should include maternal necropsy, fetal body weights, and external, visceral, and skeletal examinations of the fetuses.

rules.rule_text: Natural delivery evaluations must include fertility index, gestation index, live birth index, and postnatal follow-up of F1 offspring through weaning, including neurodevelopmental screening.
rules.verbatim_quote: Natural delivery evaluations should include fertility index, gestation index, live birth index, and postnatal follow-up of F1 offspring through weaning, including neurodevelopmental screening.

rules.rule_text: Immunological endpoints are required in DART studies for vaccines; maternal serum antibody titers must be measured at pre-dose and post-dose timepoints to confirm immune response in the dam.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunological endpoints should be included in DART studies for vaccines. Maternal serum antibody titers should be measured at pre-dose and post-dose timepoints to confirm that an immune response was generated in the dam.

rules.rule_text: Cord blood samples must be collected to assess placental transfer of vaccine-induced maternal antibodies to the fetus.
rules.verbatim_quote: Cord blood samples should be collected to assess the placental transfer of vaccine-induced maternal antibodies to the fetus.

rules.rule_text: Pup antibody levels must be measured at weaning to evaluate persistence of passively transferred maternal antibodies in the offspring.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pup antibody levels should be measured at weaning to evaluate the persistence of passively transferred maternal antibodies in the offspring.

rules.rule_text: If adverse developmental effects are observed in a DART study, additional assessments must be conducted to fully characterize the nature, dose-response, and reversibility of the effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: If adverse developmental effects are observed, additional assessments should be conducted to fully characterize the nature, dose-response, and reversibility of the effect.
rules.exceptions: Triggered only if adverse effects are observed.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine formulation used in DART studies must be the same lot as used in the clinical trial, or a comparable lot manufactured under cGMP conditions.
rules.verbatim_quote: The vaccine formulation used in the developmental toxicity study should be the same lot as that used in the clinical trial, or a comparable lot manufactured under current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) conditions.
rules.exceptions: A comparable cGMP lot is acceptable if the exact clinical trial lot is not available.

rules.rule_text: For vaccines in the same product class (e.g., similar platform technology, antigen delivery mechanism), prior DART data from a related product may be referenced on a case-by-case basis; early CBER dialogue is required to determine applicability.
rules.verbatim_quote: For vaccines in the same product class... prior developmental toxicity data from a related product may be applicable on a case-by-case basis. Sponsors should consult with CBER early to determine whether existing data are sufficient.
rules.exceptions: Applicability is determined case-by-case; cannot be assumed without CBER agreement.

rules.rule_text: ICH S5A guidance applies to vaccine DART studies but must be tailored to the unique characteristics of vaccines; sponsors must consult with CBER early in development to discuss the appropriate application of ICH S5A to their specific vaccine.
rules.verbatim_quote: ICH S5A guidance is applicable to DART studies for vaccines, but the guidance should be tailored to the unique characteristics of vaccines. Sponsors are encouraged to consult with CBER early in development to discuss the appropriate application of ICH S5A to their specific vaccine.
rules.exceptions: Tailoring of ICH S5A is required; direct application without CBER consultation is discouraged.

rules.rule_text: All DART studies for vaccines must be conducted in compliance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations under 21 CFR Part 58.
rules.verbatim_quote: Developmental toxicity studies for vaccines should be conducted in compliance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations (21 CFR Part 58).

rules.rule_text: Vaccine product labeling must include information about available developmental toxicity data in the Pregnancy subsection of the labeling (21 CFR §201.57(f)(6)).
rules.verbatim_quote: Vaccine product labeling should include information about available developmental toxicity data in the Pregnancy subsection of the labeling, as required by 21 CFR §201.57(f)(6).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must engage CBER in early dialogue (pre-IND meeting recommended) to discuss the DART study design, timing, and applicability of product class data before committing to a study plan.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors are strongly encouraged to engage in early dialogue with CBER... to discuss the DART study design, timing, and applicability of product class data before committing to a study plan. A pre-IND meeting is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Neurodevelopmental screening of F1 offspring must be included in DART studies for vaccines intended for use in females of childbearing potential, assessing motor activity, sensory function, and learning/memory.
rules.verbatim_quote: Neurodevelopmental screening of F1 offspring should be included, assessing motor activity, sensory function, and learning and memory.

rules.rule_text: DART study protocols and reports must be submitted to the IND; for maternal immunization vaccines, the completed report must be submitted and reviewed by FDA prior to enrolling pregnant women.
rules.source_section: IV. Timing and III. General Principles
rules.verbatim_quote: Developmental toxicity data should be submitted to the IND and reviewed before pregnant women are enrolled in clinical trials [for maternal immunization vaccines].
rules.exceptions: For non-maternal immunization vaccines, DART data may be submitted with the BLA rather than the IND.

rules.rule_text: Therapeutic vaccines for infectious diseases are subject to the same DART requirements as preventive vaccines when they target populations that include females of childbearing potential.
rules.verbatim_quote: This guidance applies to both preventive and therapeutic vaccines for infectious disease indications when they are intended for use in females of childbearing potential.
rules.exceptions: Vaccines exclusively for males or post-menopausal women are outside scope.
